title: Solvent Extraction Behaviour of Lead(Il) with Versatic 911
author: UDAY SANKAR RAY
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Visva. Bharati, Santiniketan-731 295 

  Manuscript received 16 May 1983, revised 29 September 1983, accepted 13 February 1984  

 Extraction of Jead(II) with liquid cation exchanger, Versatic 911, in benzene, toluene or  n -hexane from 0.1  M  acetate solution has been investigated. Qantitative extraction has been achieved in the pH range 5.5-6.0. NitriC acid (2  M ) is used for stripping the metal ion from the organic phase. The effect of pH,, extractant concentration, acetate ion concentration and contact time on extractlon of lead has been studied. The role of different diluents on extraction at different pH are discussed. The probable composition of the extracted species, PbR 2 . 2RH (RH=Versatic 911) is derived from the extraction results.
